title: The Talent Show Trick
story_html:
Leo and Dad drove to Pine Lake early one morning for their first camping trip. Leo felt so excited he could barely sit still in the car.

At the campsite, Dad handed Leo a small silver key. "This opens my old tackle box," he said. Inside were unique lures shaped like tiny frogs.

They walked down to the dock alone while the rest of the family set up the tent. Dad hoped they might catch a fish for dinner, so they cast their lines into the water. He warned Leo to hold the rod tight so it would not slip away. Soon Leo felt a strong tug, and after a happy pull, he lifted something heavy and silver out of the water.

By the fire, Dad began to prepare lunch. He let Leo stir the pot with a wooden spoon while he chopped vegetables. A rich smell drifted through the campsite, and Leo's stomach rumbled.

Then, by accident, Leo's elbow knocked the pot, and soup spilled onto the table. He worried Dad would be mad, but Dad only smiled and wiped the table dry. He was always thoughtful that way, keeping things calm.

After lunch, Leo pulled a folding chair close to the water and rested, watching the ripples change color as clouds drifted past.

That evening, families gathered around a small wooden stage for a talent show. Each camper could share something funny or clever. Leo decided to try a card trick he had practiced, though the shuffling steps still seemed to confuse his fingers.

Dad squeezed his hand to encourage him, so Leo walked up. He asked a boy to touch one card, then, with a snap, it appeared back on top. The crowd clapped, surprised.

Later, Dad said the show should include every camper next year, and that Leo's act deserved a title of its own: "The Frog Lure Magician." Before bed, Leo helped throw a few logs on the fire, then checked the campsite map, already dreaming about tomorrow.

warn.def: To tell someone about a possible danger.

share.def: To use or have something together with others.

stage.def: A raised platform where people perform.

trick.def: Something done to fool or surprise someone.

change.def: To become different, or to make something different.

encourage.def: To give someone confidence to do something.

include.def: To make someone or something part of a group.

key.def: A tool for opening a lock, or the most important part of something.

mad.def: Very angry, or (informally) a little crazy.

smell.def: To notice something using your nose.

unique.def: Being the only one of its kind; very special.

touch.def: To put your hand on something.

chair.def: A piece of furniture for one person to sit on.

title.def: The name of a book, story, or piece of work.

throw.def: To send something through the air with your hand.

early.def: Near the beginning of a period of time.

fish.def: An animal that lives and swims in water.

morning.def: The early part of the day.

confuse.def: To make someone unable to understand clearly.

thoughtful.def: Kind and caring, or deep in thought.

spoon.def: A tool with a small bowl shape, used for eating or stirring.

excited.def: Feeling very happy and full of energy about something.

accident.def: Something that happens by chance, often unplanned.

map.def: A picture that shows a place.

questions.qtext: In "He worried Dad would be mad, but Dad only smiled and wiped the table dry", what does mad mean?
questions.explain: B — Leo expected his dad to be angry about the spilled soup, but Dad stayed calm instead.

questions.qtext: In "Inside were unique lures shaped like tiny frogs", what does unique mean?
questions.explain: C — unique means special and unlike anything else, which is why Leo had never seen lures like them before.

questions.qtext: In "That evening, families gathered around a small wooden stage for a talent show", what does stage mean?
questions.explain: A — a stage is a raised platform where people perform, which is where the campers did their acts.

questions.qtext: Why did Leo's fingers get confused during his card trick?
options: He forgot the words to say
The shuffling steps were hard to do smoothly
The stage was too dark to see
questions.explain: B — the story says the shuffling steps still seemed to confuse his fingers, showing the trick was tricky to perform.

questions.qtext: How did Leo feel about the camping trip and talent show by the end of the story?
options: Bored and ready to go home
Happy and excited about the day and about tomorrow
Angry that his dad was too busy
questions.explain: C — Leo caught a fish, performed his trick successfully, and fell asleep dreaming happily about the next day.
